subject: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane
summary: Quick note from our side: the acceptance window for the Helix pilot is at risk. Two items popped in the last 48 hours that shift our internal go/no-go: a delayed dataset delivery from our analytics team and one outstanding security checklist item that InfoSec wants clarified before they sign off.
text:
Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

From: Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Aditya —

Quick note from our side: the acceptance window for the Helix pilot is at risk. Two items popped in the last 48 hours that shift our internal go/no-go: a delayed dataset delivery from our analytics team and one outstanding security checklist item that InfoSec wants clarified before they sign off.

We need a concrete plan by EOD Wednesday that shows:
- A revised acceptance schedule with firm milestone dates
- Any service/uptime concessions Redwood can offer if the final acceptance slips beyond our target
- Clear ownership for each failure mode (who at Redwood will own the remediation)

I attached our current MAP (MAP_v2.xlsx) and the redlined SOW we received from procurement last week. Can you confirm whether Redwood can do a short canary/partial-acceptance to keep our clinical integrations moving?

Thanks — please loop legal/procurement as needed.

Caroline Parker
VP Partnerships, Helix Health
caroline.parker@helixhealth.com

Attachments: MAP_v2.xlsx, SOW_Helix_redlines.pdf

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jul 2025 11:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Caroline — thanks for the heads up and for the materials. We reviewed the MAP and the SOW redlines. High level response below; I’ll put a compact, executable plan in a follow-up with dates.

Short answers:
- Canary/partial acceptance: yes — we can stage a limited acceptance for the analytics integration (read-only traffic for 2 endpoints) to enable your clinical team to continue integration work while the dataset delivery is remedied.
- SLA credits: we can offer a short-term, targeted SLA credit for any downstream outage attributable to Redwood during the extended acceptance window (details below). We’ll need a signed amendment to the SOW to codify this.

Immediate asks for the plan:
1) Confirm which analytics dataset files are delayed and ETA (so I can sequence our validation tasks)
2) Confirm which InfoSec question is outstanding (we saw the checklist item re: logging retention; if that’s it, we have a compliance note and a cert we can produce)

I’m attaching a proposed timeline CSV (timeline_proposal.csv) and I’ve dropped a working MAP folder here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_MAPs. I’ll draft a re-baselined timeline with contingency triggers and a small risk log by tomorrow morning.

Aditya Rao
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: timeline_proposal.csv

On Mon, Jul 7, 2025 at 09:12 AM Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com> wrote:
> Aditya —
>
> Quick note from our side: the acceptance window for the Helix pilot is at risk. Two items popped in the last 48 hours that shift our internal go/no-go: a delayed dataset delivery from our analytics team and one outstanding security checklist item that InfoSec wants clarified before they sign off.
>
> We need a concrete plan by EOD Wednesday that shows:
> - A revised acceptance schedule with firm milestone dates
> - Any service/uptime concessions Redwood can offer if the final acceptance slips beyond our target
> - Clear ownership for each failure mode (who at Redwood will own the remediation)
>
> I attached our current MAP (MAP_v2.xlsx) and the redlined SOW we received from procurement last week. Can you confirm whether Redwood can do a short canary/partial-acceptance to keep our clinical integrations moving?
>
> Thanks — please loop legal/procurement as needed.
>
> Caroline Parker
> VP Partnerships, Helix Health
> caroline.parker@helixhealth.com
>
> Attachments: MAP_v2.xlsx, SOW_Helix_redlines.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jul 2025 13:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Looping in a tactical option from an ops perspective — this is a playable approach we’ve used before to protect core integration timelines without an all-or-nothing acceptance:

Proposal (short):
- Phase 1 (Canary): Limited traffic to non-critical read endpoints. Validation window: 7 days. Owner: Redwood DevOps (Marissa).
- Phase 2 (Partial Acceptance): Expand to write endpoints for a subset of accounts (Helix dev cluster). Validation window: 14 days. Owner: Customer Success (Laura).
- Phase 3 (Final Acceptance): Full production traffic once dataset and InfoSec items closed. Owner: Aditya / Helix PM.

Risk log entries (initial):
- R1: Dataset delay causes regression in analytics pipeline. Mitigation: Redwood will run a parallel validation harness and report daily diffs (owner: Engineering).
- R2: Logging retention question impacts audit trail acceptance. Mitigation: Provide a signed compliance addendum and logs export mechanism.

If Helix agrees to the staged acceptance, we can keep your clinical integration schedule moving and reduce the business impact of a hard slip. I’ll draft concrete milestone dates in the timeline aditya referenced.

Marissa Cole
Director, Solutions Operations, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwoodinference.com

On Mon, Jul 7, 2025 at 11:06 AM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Caroline — thanks for the heads up and for the materials. We reviewed the MAP and the SOW redlines. High level response below; I’ll put a compact, executable plan in a follow-up with dates.
>
> Short answers:
> - Canary/partial acceptance: yes — we can stage a limited acceptance for the analytics integration (read-only traffic for 2 endpoints) to enable your clinical team to continue integration work while the dataset delivery is remedied.
> - SLA credits: we can offer a short-term, targeted SLA credit for any downstream outage attributable to Redwood during the extended acceptance window (details below). We’ll need a signed amendment to the SOW to codify this.
>
> Immediate asks for the plan:
> 1) Confirm which analytics dataset files are delayed and ETA (so I can sequence our validation tasks)
> 2) Confirm which InfoSec question is outstanding (we saw the checklist item re: logging retention; if that’s it, we have a compliance note and a cert we can produce)
>
> I’m attaching a proposed timeline CSV (timeline_proposal.csv) and I’ve dropped a working MAP folder here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_MAPs. I’ll draft a re-baselined timeline with contingency triggers and a small risk log by tomorrow morning.
>
> Aditya Rao
> Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
> aditya@redwoodinference.com
>
> Attachments: timeline_proposal.csv

From: Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>, Thomas Reed <t.reed@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Jul 2025 09:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Thanks, Aditya and Marissa — this staged path sounds promising. A couple of clarifications before we sign off on the canary plan:

1) Dates: we need Phase 1 to start no later than Monday 7/14 to avoid a cascade into clinical sign-off.
2) SLA credits: procurement needs a proposal for how the credit would be calculated (percent credit per hour of impacted uptime tied to the acceptance window) and whether that requires a pricing concession elsewhere.
3) Legal: our counsel (Thomas) will need an amendment draft to review — tagging him on this thread.

Procurement is fine to consider an acceptance amendment if Redwood can commit to the partial rollout dates and a clear list of owners for each trigger. Can you provide a short decision matrix for the triggers? Also we received SOW_Helix_redlines.pdf which includes a few contract terms we can’t accept as-is (mainly around data residency and indemnity caps). We’ll share redlines to DocuSign for tracking.

Caroline Parker
VP Partnerships, Helix Health

On Mon, Jul 7, 2025 at 01:30 PM Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Looping in a tactical option from an ops perspective — this is a playable approach we’ve used before to protect core integration timelines without an all-or-nothing acceptance:
>
> Proposal (short):
> - Phase 1 (Canary): Limited traffic to non-critical read endpoints. Validation window: 7 days. Owner: Redwood DevOps (Marissa).
> - Phase 2 (Partial Acceptance): Expand to write endpoints for a subset of accounts (Helix dev cluster). Validation window: 14 days. Owner: Customer Success (Laura).
> - Phase 3 (Final Acceptance): Full production traffic once dataset and InfoSec items closed. Owner: Aditya / Helix PM.
>
> Risk log entries (initial):
> - R1: Dataset delay causes regression in analytics pipeline. Mitigation: Redwood will run a parallel validation harness and report daily diffs (owner: Engineering).
> - R2: Logging retention question impacts audit trail acceptance. Mitigation: Provide a signed compliance addendum and logs export mechanism.

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>, Thomas Reed <t.reed@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Jul 2025 14:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Caroline / Thomas / Procurement —

Attached is a compact decision matrix and re-baselined milestone set (see timeline_proposal.csv in the MAP folder). Summary here:

Milestones (proposed):
- M0: Dataset ETA confirmation — 2025-07-10 (owner: Helix Analytics)
- M1: Phase 1 Canary start (read-only) — 2025-07-14 (owner: Redwood DevOps)
- M2: Canary validation complete — 2025-07-20 (owner: Redwood Engineering)
- M3: Phase 2 Partial acceptance start — 2025-07-22 (owner: Customer Success)
- M4: Final acceptance target — 2025-08-05 (owner: Aditya / Helix PM)

Contingency triggers (examples):
- T1: If dataset ETA slips >72 hours -> delay Phase 1 by same delta and initiate daily syncs.
- T2: If InfoSec outstanding items not closed by 7/16 -> run audit export and provide compliance addendum; do not progress to Phase 2 until resolved.

SLA credit framing: Redwood can offer a pro-rated credit for production incidents attributable to Redwood within the extended acceptance window. Draft language: ‘If Final Acceptance extends beyond agreed M4 date due to Redwood-attributable outage impacting Helix production integration, Redwood will apply a credit equal to X% of monthly service fees per hour of incident up to a cap of Y%.’ Thomas — can your legal team confirm whether you need specific numeric placeholders or a mechanism (e.g., formula) in the amendment?

Next steps:
1) Helix confirms dataset ETA by 7/10 (M0)
2) Redwood shares amendment draft (SOW addendum) with SLA credit framing by end of day 7/9 — I’ll circulate a baseline draft to DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/Docs/someDocId
3) If Helix agrees, we sign amendment and start Phase 1 on 7/14.

I’ll consolidate the risk log and ownership table into the MAP spreadsheet tonight.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: timeline_proposal.csv, MAP_v2.xlsx

From: Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Thomas Reed <t.reed@helixhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 9 Jul 2025 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Aditya / Caroline —

Procurement reviewed the proposed milestones and the SLA-credit framing. A few items from procurement/legal perspective:

1) Indemnity / data residency redlines remain blocker points; we can sign a limited amendment for the staged acceptance but the SOW must note the outstanding legal items and an escrow for final commercial acceptance.
2) For SLA credits: we need a clear formula (percent of monthly fee * hours impacted) and a cap. Please add a worked example to the amendment (e.g., $X/month -> Y% credit per hour up to Z% cap).
3) We’ll route the amendment to legal — Thomas will lead comments. Expect formal redlines back via DocuSign by Thursday EOD.

If Redwood can accept the limited amendment approach (stage acceptance + capture open legal items in an escrow clause) procurement will not block the canary start on 7/14. We’ll confirm dataset ETA separately (Caroline is working that).

Procurement Helix
procurement@helixhealth.com

On Tue, Jul 8, 2025 at 02:18 PM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com> wrote:

From: Thomas Reed <t.reed@helixhealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Procurement Helix <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Caroline Parker <c.parker@helixhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 9 Jul 2025 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance runway bump & escalation lane

Aditya / team —

We’ll review the amendment and return redlines via DocuSign. Procurement’s escrow approach is acceptable for the staged acceptance so long as the SOW explicitly lists outstanding legal items and a path to resolution with deadlines. Please include the SLA-credit formula with a worked example in the amendment under section 7.3 so our finance team can sign off.

I’ll aim to get comments back by Thursday EOD. If Redwood needs an expedited review, reply-all and I’ll flag with our counsel.

Thomas Reed
General Counsel, Helix Health

subject: Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot
summary: Thanks for taking time today, Ellen and Roberto — good to kick off the sizing conversation. Attached is a small template (lighthouse-traffic-sample.csv) — could you confirm the sample rows are representative (we're focusing on the weekday peak window and typical session shapes)?
text:
Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Ellen Park <ellen@lighthouse.ai>, Roberto Diaz <roberto@lighthouse.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot

Thanks for taking time today, Ellen and Roberto — good to kick off the sizing conversation. Attached is a small template (lighthouse-traffic-sample.csv) — could you confirm the sample rows are representative (we're focusing on the weekday peak window and typical session shapes)?

High-level asks from our side to make the Dedicated sizing tight:
- Peak concurrent sessions and how they map to user vs batch traffic
- Typical prompt token length and average response token length (sample percentiles if you have them)
- Burst windows: expected duration and recurrence (e.g., 6x spikes per day, 15-30m each)
- Session affinity requirements (sticky KV-cache across requests?)
- Any regulatory restrictions that require tenant isolation vs shared tenant pools

Based on our quick read: if your median request is ~120 prompt tokens / 420 response tokens and you want 99th latency < 500ms for 8 concurrent interactive sessions per GPU, we estimate a 1:12 occupancy at 80% aggressive batching for an A100-style GPU equivalent. But that is a rough starting point — can you validate the token mix and if you have P50/P95 response lengths?

I've cc'd Marissa (capacity lead) and Karthik (pre-sales rules) — they may follow up with a short questionnaire. Next step proposal: you send a filled sample CSV + a short paragraph describing sticky-state requirements and burst cadence; we'll return a draft tenantization map and suggested GPU-mix within 48 hours.

Attachment: lighthouse-traffic-sample.csv (CSV stub, 8 rows)

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Head of Solutions — Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0123

From: Roberto Diaz <roberto@lighthouse.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ellen Park <ellen@lighthouse.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot

Thanks Aditya — attached is a quick fill of the sample rows and some notes (see towered rows 1-8). Quick answers inline:
- Peak concurrent sessions: ~1,800 active web sessions across our US fleet during PST morning peak. Of those, ~15% are heavy-interactive (multi-turn assistant flows), rest are lighter classification/embedding calls.
- Token mix: median prompt ~95 tokens, median response ~260 tokens. P95 response ~1,600 tokens (long report synth).
- Bursts: 3 predictable spikes during 08:00-10:00 PST (each ~20-40m), plus an irregular evening spike.
- Session affinity: for multi-turn flows we prefer sticky sessions for KV-cache retention across 4-6 requests. For stateless classification calls we can tolerate any node.
- Isolation: we prefer soft tenantization (logical separation, shared GPU pools) for cost reasons but need ability to opt certain customers into strict isolation (dedicated tenant pool) for compliance.

We included a short diagram of tenant groups in tenant-map-v1.pdf (attachment). Let us know if you want anonymized sample traces — we can produce a day-of-week trace.

Roberto
CTO, Lighthouse AI
roberto@lighthouse.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Roberto Diaz <roberto@lighthouse.ai>, Ellen Park <ellen@lighthouse.ai>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot

Appreciate the quick data, Roberto — that's really helpful. A few follow-ups to tighten the GPU-fit: 
1) For the P95 1,600 token responses: is that mostly a small fraction of sessions (e.g., <1% of total requests) or do certain tenant groups produce this frequently? If it's heavy only for a named tenant, we can recommend targeted isolated pools.
2) For the 1,800 concurrent sessions: can you split that by route type (interactive assistant vs stateless inferencing vs embeddings)? Even coarse splits (e.g., 18% interactive, 60% classification, 22% embeddings) will direct which GPU SKUs are best.
3) Are you using client-side retries or long polling that might create phantom concurrency? Any guidance on retry backoff will affect occupancy.

Operational constraints to call out from Redwood side:
- If you need sticky KV-cache across requests and want 99th latency SLAs, we assume session pooling with affinity (session pinned to a GPU runtime for up to 10 minutes after last request).
- For a hybrid approach (shared pools + isolated tenants), we typically recommend a baseline shared pool sized for 80% traffic and 1-2 dedicated tenant pools for the largest 1-3 customers.

I'll draft a tenantization sketch after the route-split and P95 frequency info — expect a short PDF + a recommended GPU mix (A100x2 vs H100x1 equivalents) by EOD Wednesday.

—Marissa
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.ai

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Roberto Diaz <roberto@lighthouse.ai>, Ellen Park <ellen@lighthouse.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 15:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Tenantization & GPU-fit consolidation for Dedicated pilot

Thanks — great inputs. Summary & next steps: 
- Action items for Lighthouse: upload the full day-of-week trace (CSV) and a short mapping of tenant IDs to workload archetype (interactive / classification / embeddings). Also flag any customer IDs that must be isolated for compliance.
- Redwood will: produce a tenantization map (PDF) recommending share vs isolate pools, and two SKU+capacity options: \"cost-efficient\" (higher multiplexing, H100x1 equivalence for majority) and \"latency-first\" (lower occupancy, mixed A100/H100 split). We'll include expected p50/p95 latency estimates and cost delta per month.
- Timeline: we'll deliver the draft tenant-map + GPU-mix options by Fri 2027-04-23 18:00 PDT. If Lighthouse can provide the CSV by EOD Thu, we'll hit the target.

One technical note for Lighthouse ops: if you run embeddings at high QPS and they are stateless, we may suggest a separate optimized embedding pool (CPU+quantized GPU) to avoid polluting KV-cache-optimized runtimes.

I'll queue a 30-min review once the draft is ready. Appreciate the collaboration — cc'ing Laura (enterprise lead) to tie in contract/commit sizing when we lock options.

Attachment: tenant-map-v1.pdf (diagram stub)

Best,
Aditya Rao
Head of Solutions — Redwood Inference

subject: Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review
summary: Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's data and personnel controls last week. As part of our SOC 2 + SIG evidence intake we need a compact package covering:
text:
Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review

From: Liz Harmon <liz@whitestone.com>
To: aditya_rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Whitestone Security Team <security@whitestone.com>, laura_bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's data and personnel controls last week. As part of our SOC 2 + SIG evidence intake we need a compact package covering:

- New-hire background-check scope and vendor used (redacted PII ok)
- Completion records for mandatory security training (last 18 months)
- Evidence of offboarding/termination deprovisioning steps tied to HR events
- Any attestations you can provide for staggered re-verification or periodic screening

Can you send a single consolidated PDF (or a Drive folder link) with the artifacts above? Our deadline for the SIG intake is 2026-09-09, so the sooner the better. If anything is partial, please flag what still needs redaction or PR review.

Best,
Liz Harmon
Security Manager — Whitestone Health

---
Replying to: security questionnaire intake (SIG)

From: aditya_rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Liz Harmon <liz@whitestone.com>
Cc: laura_bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, rafael_mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review

Liz — thanks for the clear list. Attaching a staged bundle and linking the Drive folder for anything too large to include inline. Quick notes up front:

1) Background checks: we use ClearCheck Partners (vendor name) for employment screening. We can provide sample redacted reports and a vendor statement; actual PII redacted per our privacy policy.
2) Training records: export attached is the training rollup (CSV) with per-employee completion dates for Security Basics, Phishing Simulation, and Data Handling (last 18 months).
3) Offboarding: included is our Offboarding Runbook excerpt and a sample termination event checklist showing access removal steps (IAM, SSO, VPN, Gerrit).
4) Re-verification: we perform role-triggered rechecks (e.g., privileged access), and periodic attestations for contractors — summary attestation included.

Attachments: onboarding-training-log-2025Q4.csv, background-check-redaction-samples.pdf
Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB3-WhT-credentialing-evidence

If you want a single compiled PDF for the SIG binder, I can ask Laura to produce a redacted packet and get sign-off from Legal. Do you need DocuSign-ready attestations or a signed letter from our HR lead?

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

---
Original message from Liz Harmon included above

From: laura_bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: rafael_mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, grace_oconnor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, aditya_rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: none
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 14:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review

Folks — forwarding Whitestone's ask. Rafael, could you generate a single redacted PDF binder (SOC2/SIG friendly) that contains:

- vendor attestation from ClearCheck (we have a template)
- three redacted sample reports (already attached in the original thread)
- training completion matrix (CSV -> appendix table)
- offboarding checklist + one filled example of a termination event
- HR attestation letter agreeing to the statements in the SIG (simple, signed PDF)

Grace — can you confirm the timeline for getting Legal to sign the HR attestation? Whitestone asked for a return by 2026-09-09; if that is tight we should communicate a partial delivery (deliver artifacts now, signed attestation next day).

Rafael — please also produce a short cover memo that states retention windows for background check reports and where those are stored. Use the Drive link Aditya provided as the drop. Mark anything that requires redaction with [REDACT]

Thanks — LA

Laura Bennett
Head of People Ops
Redwood Inference

From: rafael_mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Liz Harmon <liz@whitestone.com>
Cc: aditya_rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, laura_bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, grace_oconnor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Sep 2026 16:38:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Credentialing & termination attestations for SOC 2 / SIG review

Hi Liz — attaching the compiled redacted packet as discussed. Contents:

1) Redwood_HR_Attestation_2026-09-02.pdf (signed)
2) onboarding-training-log-2025Q4.csv (exported from LMS)
3) background-check-redaction-samples.pdf (three redacted samples)
4) offboarding-checklist-sample.pdf (runbook excerpt + example)
5) retention-and-storage-memo.txt (covers retention windows & storage locations)

Drive folder (same): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB3-WhT-credentialing-evidence
DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/xyz/packet/REDWOOD-HR-ATTEST-2026-09

Notes:
- Background reports are redacted to last four of SSN and remove DOB. Full reports are retained by ClearCheck for 5 years per our agreement; we provide samples only.
- Training CSV maps employee_id -> role -> course -> completion_date; employee_id is internal numeric key (no PII).
- Offboarding steps are automated via the HRIS webhook; checklist includes verification timestamp for each action.

If you need any clarifications or a notarized letter, let me know and I can escalate.

Best regards,
Rafael Mendes
People Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Redwood_HR_Attestation_2026-09-02.pdf; onboarding-training-log-2025Q4.csv; background-check-redaction-samples.pdf; offboarding-checklist-sample.pdf; retention-and-storage-memo.txt
